blocks.content:
**k6 Script Authoring** is a dedicated mode of Grafana Assistant focused on performance tests. General Assistant chat helps across Grafana; this mode steers the model toward k6 best practices—**checks** (response assertions), **thresholds** (metric pass or fail gates), **URL grouping** (stable metric names instead of one time series per raw URL), realistic **stages** or executors, and optional **Tempo** or **Pyroscope** hooks—so the output is ready to review in Grafana Cloud k6. Refer to **Terms used in this path** on the path cover for short definitions and doc links.

Switch into this mode whenever you want to **create** a new test from language or telemetry, **improve** an existing k6 script, or **convert** artifacts such as Postman or curl into k6.

## Open k6 Script Authoring and pick a workflow

To open Grafana Assistant in **k6 Script Authoring** mode, complete the following steps:

1. On the main menu, click **Testing & synthetics** > **Performance**.
2. Click the **Assistant** icon at the top of the sidebar to open Grafana Assistant.
3. In the mode selector at the bottom of the Assistant panel, choose **k6 Script Authoring**.
4. When Assistant asks what you want to do, use the **Follow-up** buttons (they match the on-screen choices): **Write new test**, **Discover & generate**, or **Work with existing script**. The chat may also label these as A, B, and C—pick the path that fits a new prompt, telemetry-backed discovery, or paste-and-improve.

Expect outputs such as a full **JavaScript k6 script**, concrete **suggestions** (for example cardinality fixes or missing checks), or a **converted** script when you supplied Postman or curl—not generic Grafana answers unrelated to load testing.
In the next milestone, you generate a complete script from a plain-language prompt and learn how to read what Assistant produced.
